product_overview: SessionFlow is a desktop and cloud-native platform for managing instructor sessions, timetables, attendance and real-time team chat. It provides secure role-based APIs for scheduling, attendance tracking, group management and administrative workflows with SignalR-backed real-time updates.
core_goals: Provide secure authentication and role-based access for engineers and admins
Enable full session lifecycle management (create, schedule, mark attendance, state transitions)
Support real-time attendance, chat and presence updates via SignalR
Offer CRUD group management for cohorts and student profiling
Expose administrative controls for approving engineers and issuing registration codes
Ensure graceful error handling and clear API responses for integration and testing

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Authenticate users, register engineers using access codes, and retrieve the current user identity.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid email/password -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> GET /api/auth/me with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 with user profile

POST /api/auth/register with name,email,password and valid accessCode (e.g. ENG1) -> Receive 201 Created with pending or active engineer response -> POST /api/auth/login with new credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token
POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 Unauthorized with error message
POST /api/auth/register with invalid or expired accessCode -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation error

features.name: Groups API
features.description: Create, list and update student groups, enabling cohorts, tags, levels and active engineer assignments.
user_flows: POST /api/groups with Authorization: Bearer <token> and valid group payload -> Receive 201 Created with group id -> GET /api/groups with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 with list containing the new group -> PUT /api/groups/{id} with Authorization: Bearer <token> and updated fields -> Receive 200 OK with updated group
GET /api/groups without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
PUT /api/groups/{id} with Authorization: Bearer <token> and nonexistent id -> Receive 404 Not Found
POST /api/groups with Authorization: Bearer <token> and invalid payload (missing required fields) -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation errors

features.name: Sessions & Attendance API
features.description: Create and list teaching sessions and mark attendance records (Present, Absent, Late) for sessions.
user_flows: POST /api/sessions with Authorization: Bearer <token> and valid session payload (groupId,start,end,engineerId) -> Receive 201 Created with session id -> GET /api/sessions with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 with the created session in list -> PUT /api/sessions/{id}/attendance with Authorization: Bearer <token> and attendance payload -> Receive 200 OK with attendance records stored
PUT /api/sessions/{id}/attendance with Authorization: Bearer <token> and malformed attendance payload -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation error
POST /api/sessions with Authorization: Bearer <token> and payload that conflicts with existing timetable -> Receive 409 Conflict indicating scheduling collision
GET /api/sessions with expired or invalid token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Real-time Hub (SignalR) — Chat & Presence
features.description: WebSocket (SignalR) hub for real-time chat messages, presence updates and session-scoped broadcasts. Clients connect with JWT via access_token query parameter.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> Open WebSocket to ws://localhost:5180/hub?access_token=<token> -> Send 'JoinSession' invocation with sessionId -> Receive 'JoinSessionConfirmed' event -> Send 'NewChatMessage' invocation with sessionId and message -> Receive broadcast 'NewChatMessage' event for session participants

POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> Open WebSocket to ws://localhost:5180/hub?access_token=<token> -> Send 'UpdatePresence' invocation with status 'online' -> Receive broadcast 'PresenceChanged' event for relevant group members
Open WebSocket to ws://localhost:5180/hub without access_token -> Connection rejected or receive 401 Unauthorized handshake error
Open WebSocket to ws://localhost:5180/hub?access_token=<token> -> Send 'JoinSession' invocation with invalid/nonexistent sessionId -> Receive error event indicating session not found

features.name: Admin Console API
features.description: Administrative endpoints for viewing engineers, creating registration access codes, and approving pending engineer registrations. Restricted to Admin-only claims.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with admin credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token containing Admin claim -> GET /api/engineers with Authorization: Bearer <admin token> -> Receive 200 with list of engineers -> POST /api/engineer-codes with Authorization: Bearer <admin token> and payload (code,expires,uses) -> Receive 201 Created with code -> PUT /api/pending/{id}/approve with Authorization: Bearer <admin token> -> Receive 200 OK confirming approval
POST /api/auth/login with non-admin credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token without Admin claim -> GET /api/engineers with Authorization: Bearer <non-admin token> -> Receive 403 Forbidden
PUT /api/pending/{id}/approve with Authorization: Bearer <admin token> and nonexistent pending id -> Receive 404 Not Found
POST /api/engineer-codes with Authorization: Bearer <admin token> and invalid payload -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation errors

endpoints.description: Register a new engineer using access code

features.name: Sessions API
features.description: Core session management and lifecycle operations
